questionText: A curve has parametric equations $x = 2t$ and $y = t^{2} - 3$. Determine whether the point $(6,\, 6)$ lies on the curve.

steps.explanation: A point lies on the curve only if a single value of $t$ produces both of its coordinates. We find $t$ from the $x$-coordinate, then test the $y$-coordinate.
Setting the expression for $x$ equal to the point's $x$-coordinate of $6$ lets us find the parameter that would give this $x$.
Dividing both sides by $2$ gives $t = 3$, the only value of $t$ that produces $x = 6$.
To test the point we work out the $y$-value the curve actually has when $t = 3$.
This matches the $y$-coordinate of the point, so both coordinates come from the same value $t = 3$. Therefore the point does lie on the curve.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: Yes — the point $(6,\,6)$ lies on the curve, at $t = 3$.
